examples.title: Basic Multiple Inheritance Example
examples.description: Simple program demonstrating multiple inheritance syntax with Person and Student classes
examples.explanation: Demonstrates multiple inheritance where CollegeStudent inherits from both Person and Student. Constructor order: Person first (first in list), then Student (second), then CollegeStudent. Destructor order is reverse.

examples.title: Diamond Problem Without Virtual Inheritance
examples.description: Program showing diamond problem - ambiguity when accessing inherited members from common base
examples.explanation: Shows diamond problem where class D has two copies of A (one through B, one through C). Accessing A's members requires scope resolution to specify which path. Both B::data and C::data are separate copies.

examples.title: Diamond Problem Solution with Virtual Inheritance
examples.description: Program demonstrating virtual inheritance to solve diamond problem - single copy of base class
examples.explanation: Virtual inheritance makes A a virtual base class, ensuring only one copy exists in D. Constructor order: Virtual base A first, then B and C (in declaration order), then D. Most derived class D is responsible for initializing A.

examples.title: Ambiguity Resolution using Scope Resolution Operator
examples.description: Program showing how to resolve ambiguity when base classes have same-named methods
examples.explanation: When multiple base classes have members with same name, direct access causes ambiguity. Use BaseClass::memberName syntax to resolve. Scope resolution operator (::) specifies exactly which base class's member to access.
